title: Contract-Bound Inference Unit Type System
subtitle: Make the standalone harness route work from explicit inference unit contracts.
scope: Standalone living-doc harness inference unit type registry, run config, routing contracts, deterministic side effects, dashboard observability, commit discipline, PR review, and post-flight summaries.
sourceCoverage: Created from GitHub epic #224 and subtickets #218-#223, #235, and #236 after the realtime dashboard run exposed that closure can currently happen without git commit discipline.
objective: Implement a contract-bound inference unit type system for the standalone living-doc harness so lifecycle decisions are no longer hard-coded as a fixed sequence of worker, reviewer, repair, and closure steps, but are selected from an explicit registry of allowed inference unit types. Each unit type must define its input contract, prompt contract, required evidence, output verdicts, allowed next unit types, deterministic side effects, dashboard representation, and closure implications. The controller must use those contracts to route work until the living doc objective is proven or the user explicitly stops the run.
successCondition: A standalone living-doc harness run can initialize from an allowed inference-unit-type configuration, invoke only registered unit types through validated input and output contracts, route dynamically from inference verdicts, execute deterministic side effects only from approved contracts, show every unit and handoff in the dashboard, require commit evidence when source files changed, route PR-review work through explicit contracts when configured, run post-flight summary after closure, and prove the full path with contract and E2E tests covering direct closure, repair routing, commit-intent gating, and PR-review routing.

runState.nextObjectiveAction: Controller must re-enter closure review with commit evidence e08bdc3, then run post-flight summary if closure is approved.

basis: Contract-bound inference unit registry, run-allowed type configuration, typed invocation metadata, route validation, side-effect contracts, dashboard graph artifacts, PR-review routing, and post-flight summary routing are implemented in source.
npm run test:e2e initially reproduced the rendered-doc drag failure, then passed after the rendered graph node drag handler was hardened and the E2E drag proof was made deterministic.
Commit evidence for the changed source set is recorded as e08bdc3 (Add contract-bound inference unit routing).
Objective closure is still not claimed because closure review and post-flight summary are controller-owned lifecycle steps after this worker exits.

objectiveFacets.name: Inference unit type registry
objectiveFacets.description: Registered unit types are the source of truth for invocation shape, contracts, prompts, evidence, verdicts, allowed next units, side effects, dashboard rendering, and closure implications.

objectiveFacets.name: Run-scoped allowed unit types
objectiveFacets.description: Each standalone lifecycle declares which registered unit types it may use so routing is powerful but bounded.

objectiveFacets.name: Contract-bound invocation
objectiveFacets.description: Every unit starts from a typed input contract and prompt contract rather than ambient controller assumptions.

objectiveFacets.name: Validated routing
objectiveFacets.description: Decision units can only select next unit types from their allowed contract set, and invalid selections become durable continuation or blocker evidence.

objectiveFacets.name: Deterministic side effects
objectiveFacets.description: Git commits and GitHub PR operations are performed by the controller only from approved inference output contracts.

objectiveFacets.name: Closure contract
objectiveFacets.description: Closure requires passing proof gates, valid source state, side-effect evidence when needed, and no unresolved objective terms or unproven acceptance criteria.

objectiveFacets.name: Dashboard observability
objectiveFacets.description: The dashboard and run narrative show unit type, allowed set, selected next unit, validation status, side effects, contracts, and handoff arrows from lifecycle artifacts.

objectiveFacets.name: Post-flight summary
objectiveFacets.description: A post-flight unit can explain a closed run from artifacts without changing closure.

invariants.statement: The lifecycle must not encode worker-reviewer-repair-closure as the only route when the objective requires selection from registered inference unit types.
The deterministic controller validates contracts, performs approved side effects, and routes; it must not semantically invent the next step outside inference output contracts.
If lifecycle artifacts show source files changed, closure must require commit evidence or an explicit no-commit exemption contract.
PR readiness, review feedback, CI status, unresolved threads, and GitHub mutations must be represented as contract evidence, not supervising-chat memory.
The lifecycle may stop only when objective closure is proven or the user explicitly stops the run.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Shows that this doc is a fresh implementation objective, not evidence that the type system already exists.

data.name: Repair iteration 2 state
data.summary: The contract-bound inference unit type system is implemented, both contract plus full E2E suites pass, and commit evidence e08bdc3 records the changed source set.
data.nextStep: Controller must re-enter closure review with commit evidence e08bdc3, then run post-flight summary if closure is approved.

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.rationale: Defines objective-tight closure gates. Each criterion maps to an accountable phrase in the objective and to one or more GitHub tickets.

data.name: Inference unit type registry exists
data.objectiveTerm: selected from an explicit registry of allowed inference unit types
data.criterion: The harness has an inference unit type registry that defines worker, reviewer, closure-review, repair/balance units, commit-intent, PR-review, and post-flight summary as explicit unit types.
data.proofRequired: A first-class registry or equivalent source defines each unit type's id, role, input contract, prompt contract, evidence requirements, output verdicts, allowed next unit types, deterministic side effects, dashboard representation, and closure implications.
data.acceptanceTest: A contract test can load the registry and assert every required unit type has complete contract metadata.
data.repairEvidence: Registry completeness is covered by scripts/living-doc-harness-inference-unit-types.mjs and living-doc harness inference unit contract tests; npm run test:contract passed.

ticketIds.title: Epic: contract-bound inference unit type system
Add inference unit type registry for living-doc harness

data.name: Standalone runs declare allowed unit types
data.objectiveTerm: registry of allowed inference unit types
data.criterion: A standalone lifecycle run is initialized with a config that declares which registered inference unit types are allowed for that run.
data.proofRequired: Lifecycle run artifacts include the allowed type set, and attempted routing outside that set is rejected.
data.acceptanceTest: A test starts a run with a restricted allowed set and proves unavailable types cannot be selected.
data.repairEvidence: Run-scoped allowed unit sets are recorded and invalid restricted selections are covered by lifecycle/controller contract tests; npm run test:contract passed.

ticketIds.title: Add standalone lifecycle run config for allowed inference unit types

data.name: Unit invocation comes from registered type definitions
data.objectiveTerm: Each unit type must define its input contract, prompt contract, required evidence, output verdicts
data.criterion: Every inference unit invocation is created from its registered type definition, including prompt shape, input contract schema, tool/MCP profile, required artifact paths, and output contract schema.
data.proofRequired: Worker, reviewer, closure-review, repair, commit-intent, PR-review, and post-flight invocations all carry type-derived contract metadata in artifacts.
data.acceptanceTest: A contract test fails if any lifecycle invocation bypasses registry metadata or writes an untyped input/output contract.
data.repairEvidence: Unit invocations carry registry-derived contract metadata and output validation in harness invocation/iteration code; npm run test:contract passed.

ticketIds.title: Make inference unit type selection explicit in contracts

data.name: Routing selections are validated
data.criterion: Decision-making inference units can only select next unit types from the allowed set declared by their type contract and the run config.
data.proofRequired: Controller validation rejects invalid next-unit selections and records the rejection as continuation or blocker evidence.
data.acceptanceTest: A fixture reviewer selects an invalid unit type; the lifecycle records validation failure and does not invoke that unit.
data.repairEvidence: Invalid next-unit selections are rejected and recorded as continuation evidence by proof-route/controller tests; npm run test:contract passed.

ticketIds.title: Handle invalid inference unit selections as continuation evidence

data.name: Deterministic side effects require approved contracts
data.criterion: Deterministic side effects, including git commits and GitHub PR operations, happen only from approved output contracts and are recorded as proof artifacts.
data.proofRequired: Commit-intent emits approved paths/message/exclusions; controller stages only those paths and records commit SHA. PR-review emits approved GitHub actions; controller records PR URL, comments, review status, and check state.
data.acceptanceTest: Tests prove dirty source changes cannot close without commit evidence, and PR mutations cannot occur without a PR-review output contract.
data.repairEvidence: Commit-intent and PR-review side-effect contracts are implemented and tested, including dirty-source commit gating. The changed source set was committed as e08bdc3 (Add contract-bound inference unit routing), providing commit SHA evidence for closure review.

ticketIds.title: Add commit-discipline inference unit and commit contract gate
Add PR-review inference unit type and review contract handling

data.name: Closure uses type-system proof gates
data.objectiveTerm: route work until the living doc objective is proven or the user explicitly stops the run
data.criterion: Closure cannot occur unless the closure-review unit receives passing proof gates, valid source state, required side-effect evidence such as commit SHA when files changed, and no unresolved objective terms or unproven acceptance criteria.
data.proofRequired: Closure artifacts include reviewer verdict, closure-review verdict, proof gates, source state, commit/PR evidence when required, and explicit no-next-iteration output.
data.acceptanceTest: A run with source changes but no commit SHA remains open; a run with commit SHA and passing gates can close.
data.repairEvidence: Closure gates reject dirty source state without commit evidence and allow closure only with passing proof gates. Commit evidence e08bdc3 is now available; objective closure still awaits controller-owned closure review.

ticketIds.title: Add commit-discipline inference unit and commit contract gate

data.name: Dashboard renders types, contracts, and side effects from artifacts
data.criterion: The dashboard renders unit types, contracts, deterministic side effects, validation status, and handoff arrows from lifecycle artifacts rather than a hard-coded sequence.
data.proofRequired: Graph artifacts contain unit type ids, allowed sets, selected next units, validation results, side-effect contracts, commit SHA and PR URL when present.
data.acceptanceTest: Dashboard contract and browser tests prove direct-closure, repair, commit-intent, and PR-review paths produce different artifact-derived graphs.
data.repairEvidence: Dashboard artifacts expose unit ids, allowed sets, selections, validation, side effects, and handoffs; focused routing E2E and full npm run test:e2e passed.

ticketIds.title: Expose inference unit selection observability in dashboard and narratives

data.name: Post-flight summary runs after closure
data.criterion: Post-flight summary runs after closure and produces a human-readable decision narrative from the same artifacts without changing closure.
data.proofRequired: Closed lifecycle artifacts show a post-flight unit invocation after terminal closure, and the summary links decisions to contracts, proofs, side effects, and raw log references.
data.acceptanceTest: A closed fixture run produces a static HTML narrative and the terminal closure artifact remains unchanged.
data.repairEvidence: Post-flight summary routing runs after terminal closure in lifecycle tests and does not mutate terminal closure artifacts; npm run test:contract passed.

ticketIds.title: Add post-flight inference unit for closed lifecycle run summaries

data.name: Required route proofs cover the abstraction
data.objectiveTerm: route work until the living doc objective is proven
data.criterion: Contract and E2E tests prove at least one run that closes directly, one run that routes through repair, one run that requires commit-intent before closure, and one run that routes into PR-review.
data.proofRequired: Four route fixtures produce lifecycle artifacts, dashboard graphs, reviewer/closure verdicts, and route-specific proof gates.
data.acceptanceTest: The focused route-proof test suite passes and fails if any required route is still hard-coded or invisible in artifacts.
data.repairEvidence: Direct closure, repair routing, commit-intent gating, and PR-review routing are covered by tests/e2e/harness-inference-unit-routing.spec.mjs; full npm run test:e2e passed.

ticketIds.title: Epic: contract-bound inference unit type system

sections.title: Relationship Maps
sections.rationale: Makes the intended control structure visible. These diagrams are not proof; the acceptance criteria define what must later be implemented and tested.

data.name: Type registry routing
data.claim: The controller routes through validated contracts selected from the registry and run allowed set, not through a fixed controller sequence.
data.validationQuestion: Can every unit invocation point back to a registered type and a run-allowed selection?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if controller code invokes a unit type not present in registry/run config or bypasses output-contract validation.

diagrams.title: Registry-Constrained Routing
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Registry["Inference unit type registry"] --> RunConfig["Run allowed type set"]
  RunConfig --> Controller["Deterministic lifecycle controller"]
  Controller --> InputContract["Typed input contract"]
  InputContract --> Unit["Inference unit"]
  Unit --> OutputContract["Typed output contract"]
  OutputContract --> Validator["Controller validation"]
  Validator -->|valid next type| Controller
  Validator -->|invalid selection| Evidence["Continuation or blocker evidence"]

data.name: Side-effect contract gates
data.claim: Source changes and PR operations become deterministic side effects only after inference produces an approved contract.
data.validationQuestion: Can every commit SHA or PR mutation be traced to an inference output contract that approved it?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if workers commit directly, broad-stage dirty files, or mutate PRs without controller-recorded contracts.

diagrams.title: Commit and PR Side-Effect Gates
diagrams.text:
flowchart TD
  Worker["Worker changes files"] --> Reviewer["Reviewer validates evidence"]
  Reviewer --> CommitIntent["commit-intent unit"]
  CommitIntent --> CommitContract["approved paths + message + exclusions"]
  CommitContract --> GitCommit["Controller stages exact paths and commits"]
  GitCommit --> CommitEvidence["commit SHA artifact"]
  CommitEvidence --> PRReview["pr-review unit"]
  PRReview --> PRContract["approved GitHub action contract"]
  PRContract --> GitHubAction["Controller opens/updates/reviews PR"]
  GitHubAction --> PREvidence["PR URL + review/check evidence"]
  CommitEvidence --> Closure["closure-review gate"]
  PREvidence --> Closure

data.name: Closure and post-flight
data.claim: Closure remains a proof decision; post-flight summary happens after closure and cannot change it.
data.validationQuestion: Does the lifecycle stop only after closure review proves the objective or after explicit user stop?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if post-flight summary becomes a closure gate or if closure happens before required side-effect evidence.

diagrams.title: Closure Boundary
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  ProofGates["Proof gates"] --> ClosureReview["closure-review unit"]
  SourceState["living doc source state"] --> ClosureReview
  SideEffects["commit / PR evidence when required"] --> ClosureReview
  ClosureReview -->|closed| Terminal["terminal closed artifact"]
  ClosureReview -->|not closed| NextUnit["next registered unit"]
  Terminal --> PostFlight["post-flight summary unit"]
  PostFlight --> Narrative["static decision narrative"]
  Narrative -. "does not alter" .-> Terminal

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.rationale: Stages the proof needed before this objective can claim completion.
